old_claim.note: Trade Routes provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains from trade_routes to proto_writing. The corrected node is scoped to Uruk-period administrative pictographic/proto-cuneiform notation, where token/accounting systems and symbolic marking are source-backed predecessors but long-distance trade routes are not a direct prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract/introduction and phase summary: clay tokens for counting and recording goods precede pictographic script; phase (b) places two-dimensional pictographic signs around 3500-3000 BC.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports a record-keeping/accounting predecessor for proto-writing, not trade-route infrastructure as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Economic administration and accounting are retained through record_keeping; trade_routes would over-broaden the causal path and imply a commercial network dependency not established by the writing-origin source.

ontology_before: The graph treated broad trade-route infrastructure as a prerequisite for proto_writing.
ontology_after: Proto_writing is tied to administrative record keeping and symbolic notation, while trade routes remain available for downstream commercial technologies where source-backed.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: trade_routes is absent as a direct prerequisite for proto_writing; record_keeping remains the source-backed administrative predecessor.
would_reject_if: A specific source established long-distance trade routes as a direct prerequisite for the scoped Uruk-period proto-writing technology.
The node were split into a broader commercial-signaling node where trade-route infrastructure was the sourced causal mechanism.
short_reason: Removes unsupported broad commercial infrastructure from the proto-writing origin path.
